plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with the sell signal.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, as per conservative mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% to boost income and align with the scanner's exit signal.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to trim broad index exposure.
Initiate a new position in AAPL at 8% for quality tech exposure.
Hold ~15% cash buffer after these moves for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% monthly, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. However, it will likely underperform in strong bull markets.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and align with the sell signal.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 15% to trim broad index exposure.
Initiate a new position in AVGO at 10% for quality chip exposure.
Initiate a new position in AAPL at 10% for quality tech exposure.
Maintain a ~10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns, balancing growth with risk management.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and align with the sell signal.
Sell all QQQI to reallocate capital towards higher growth opportunities.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to trim broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL, reallocating from underperforming leveraged ETFs.
Initiate a new position in LEU at 15% due to its high scanner score.
Initiate a new position in GUSH at 10% for its strong risk/reward and accumulate signal.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% monthly, aiming for stretched growth. It embraces higher concentration and leverages strong scanner setups. Expect significantly larger drawdowns and potential for rapid losses due to leverage decay and market volatility. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with the sell signal.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income generation.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to trim broad index exposure.
Sell all SMH and CRWD to eliminate high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove all leveraged ETF exposure.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for stability and income reinvestment.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% monthly, prioritizing steady distributions. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged holdings in favor of income ETFs. While aiming for lower drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
